properties.description: From emergency sewer construction to safety and streetscape improvements, the diversity of projects presented in Rufus King Park gives a great opportunity for inter-agency collaboration.
The playground, basketball court, and clay pipe reconstruction gives a great opportunity for the integrated water management under the playground and the newly reconstructed pipes.

The skatepark is located next to an extensive resurfacing project mamaged by DDC, which highlights an opportunity for reconstructing the skatepark as basin, or possible connection to the existing sewer systems in the area.

A DOT project on constructing Ozone Park Plaza significantly overlaps with many green infrastructure facilities that DEP provided, along with the reconstruction of the clay piped water by DDC, and a nearby pedestrian plaza constructed by DPR. This intersection underscores an opportunity for integrated water management.
New public spaces, green infrastructures, and pedestrian upgrading highlights the opportunity for reconsideration of intergrated water management on the site.

The clusters of DEP green infrastructure projects intersect with Brownsville pedestrian safety improvement, along with the park itself highlight the opportunities for integrated water management consideration.

The presence of the public space, green infrastructures, along with street improvement for safety from DOT and DDC emphasizes the need for inter-agency planning to integrate public space and integrated water management into a single, effective system.

The playground is located next to major green infrastructure renovation organized by DDC, the Avenue J improvement by DOT, and many DEP's green infrastructure projects should be under consideration for a more sustainable intergrated water management.

The park, playground, upgraded sewer, and municipal parking field in close proximity offers a great opportunity in providing integrated water management systems with connected sewer, smart basin, and flood-resilient infrastructure.

The Marine Park is located next to the Bay, and is located next to the park connector (managed by DOT) and DDC's pipe water reconstruction highlights a pressing need for integrated water management that are flood-resilient and sustainable in a long run.
The park, pedestrian access, and citywide resurfacing provides opportunities to consider implementing integrated water management.
